toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to run tests and collect crash dumps if a test crashes the host process. In .NET, you can use the `--crashdump` or `--blame-crash` options to collect crash dumps.

Since this project uses `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` (Microsoft.Testing.Platform), the crash dump collection is done via `--crashdump` flag (for the new testing platform).

Let me run the tests with crash dump collection enabled.

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-6d66bcae5b814aeb951284e72e509fcf && dotnet test --crashdump 2>&1", "description": "Run dotnet test with crash dump collection", "initial_wait": 60}

toolArgs.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crashdump via -- separator

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with crashdump via -- separator

data.reasoningText:
The `--crashdump` flag isn't being recognized properly by MSBuild. This project uses `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`, which means it uses the newer Microsoft.Testing.Platform integration. The crash dump option needs to be passed differently.

For the new testing platform with `dotnet test`, you pass platform-specific options after `--`. Let me try:

```
dotnet test -- --crashdump
```

Or we could use the blame-crash approach with the older vstest integration. But since this project explicitly sets `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport=true`, it's using the new testing platform.


I'll pass the option after the `--` separator to see if that works with the testing platform.

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crashdump via -- separator

toolArgs.description: Run dotnet test with crashdump via -- separator

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. Here's the command:

```
dotnet test -- --crashdump
```

Since your project uses `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`, the `--crashdump` flag is a **Microsoft.Testing.Platform** option and must be passed after the `--` separator so `dotnet test` forwards it to the test host. If a test crashes the host process, a dump file will be written to the test results directory.
